title: artemisia pseudogallica
intro: Artemisia pseudogallica, also known as False Gallic Wormwood, is a perennial plant in the daisy family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. Traditionally, Artemisia pseudogallica has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its antibacterial and antifungal properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce inflammation, alleviate digestive issues, and even help to lower fever. The herbal preparations of Artemisia pseudogallica include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
